name: AgentSec (agentsec-hook-pack)
description: Runtime security and policy enforcement hooks for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex). Intercepts and classifies risky tool calls (destructive commands, file edits, secret access, production deploys) in real time before execution.
keyFeatures: Zero-configuration quickstart: drop the hook .agentsec folder into any repository
Fast-path local check: allowed safe actions (ls, grep, pwd, npm test) pass instantly without network latency
Remote policy validation: risky actions are classified by a central policy engine
Human-in-the-loop approval: dangerous commands generate a persistent approval gate URL and wait for reviewer actions
Dual agent integration: supports Claude Code (settings.json PreToolUse hook) and OpenAI Codex (config.toml PreToolUse hook)
architecture:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Agent as AI Agent (Claude/Codex)
  participant Hook as agentsec-hook.mjs
  participant API as AgentSec API
  participant Human as Human Reviewer
  
  Agent->>Hook: PreToolUse event (Bash / Edit / Write)
  Hook->>Hook: Local safe-command check
  alt Safe command (ls, grep)
    Hook-->>Agent: allow (skip network)
  else Risky command
    Hook->>API: POST tool payload + agentId
    API->>API: Risk classification
    alt Policy Allow
      API-->>Hook: decision: allow
      Hook-->>Agent: allow
    else Policy Review
      API-->>Hook: decision: requires_approval
      Hook-->>Agent: pause + show approval URL
      Human->>API: Approve / Reject
      Agent->>Hook: Retry after approval
    else Policy Denied
      API-->>Hook: decision: deny
      Hook-->>Agent: block command execution
    end
  end
fileStructure: .agentsec/hooks/agentsec-hook.mjs - Primary hook runtime execution script
.agentsec/config.json - Policy configuration (baseUrl, safeCommands, failClosedFor, mode: observe/enforce)
.claude/settings.agentsec.example.json - Claude Code hook registration preset
.codex/config.agentsec.example.toml - OpenAI Codex hook configuration template
docs/AGENTSEC_HOOK_PACK.md - Deployment and integration manuals
details: AgentSec guards against destructive bash commands (e.g. rm -rf, DROP TABLE, git push --force), unreviewed file writes (like .env edits, production settings), and secret keychain lookups. The config.json allows setting modes: 'observe' mode reports events without blocking, while 'enforce' mode blocks untrusted commands. Safe commands like 'pwd', 'npm test', 'npm run lint' bypass the server entirely to conserve network round-trips.

name: agent-cli-mcp-rust
description: High-performance Model Context Protocol (MCP) server written in Rust that acts as a unified control plane orchestrating six different AI execution engines.
keyFeatures: Unified interface: manages GitHub Copilot, Google Jules, Gemini, Codex, OpenCode, and Claude CLIs
Tool permission profiles: scopes command levels (e.g. copilot-file-edit, copilot-safe-dev, copilot-target-repo)
Directory isolation: strict allowedRoots check blocks executions outside specified development folders
Secret scrubbing: real-time regex scanner censors API keys, JWTs, Stripe tokens, and DB credentials from stdout
Mandatory deny overlay: hard-coded security layers block destructive calls like 'vercel deploy --prod' or force pushes
architecture:
graph TD
  CC[Claude Code] -->|JSON-RPC over stdio| MCP[MCP Transport]
  MCP --> POL[Directory Policy]
  MCP --> RED[Scrubber Engine]
  MCP --> SES[Session Manager]
  SES -->|pipes| Executors[Copilot CLI / Jules / Gemini]
fileStructure: src/main.rs - Entry point, MCP transport, and stdio JSON-RPC loop
src/server.rs - Tool registration and handler mappings
src/policy.rs - Path validation and allowed roots checker
src/redact.rs - Regex secret scrubbing engine
src/executors/ - Submodules for each CLI (copilot.rs, jules.rs, gemini.rs)
config.json - Default server setup (allowedRoots, stateDir, defaultTimeoutMs)
details: The server exposes multiple MCP tools: 'agent_cli.overview' gives a session status board; 'agent_cli.capabilities' checks CLI versions and flags; 'agent_cli.run' triggers quick synchronous executions; 'agent_cli.start_run' and 'agent_cli.start_session' support long-running interactive tasks. Jules-specific tools ('jules.create_session', 'jules.approve_plan') provide fine-grained controls over remote Google AI development runs.

name: antigravity-skill-injector
description: A dynamic skill loader for the Antigravity Desktop App. Solves system prompt context bloat by replacing full SKILL.md documents with 20-token stub files, loading the actual instructions dynamically on demand via a Rust MCP server.
keyFeatures: Saves up to 97% conversation tokens by replacing multi-hundred-line skills with tiny 20-token stubs
Rust MCP Server: ultra-fast JSON-RPC stdio server that serves full skill contents instantly when requested
Python migration script: parses frontmatter of SKILL.md files and serializes them into a centralized JSON registry
Python stub generator: creates minimal stub files for the Antigravity desktop client UI
Bash installer: compiles Rust, migrates registry, creates stubs, and outputs config blocks in one command
architecture:
graph LR
  UI[Antigravity UI] -->|Renders stubs| Stub[skills/stub.md]
  Agent[Agent Context] -->|needs skill| MCP[Rust MCP Server]
  MCP -->|reads| Reg[skills_registry.json]
  Reg -->|full payload| Agent
fileStructure: src/mcp-server/ - Rust stdio MCP server compiling to skill-injector-mcp
scripts/migrate_skills.py - YAML frontmatter parser and JSON compiler
scripts/generate_stubs.py - Autogeneration script for token-saving stubs
install.sh - One-key setup script
skills_registry.json - Centralized skill instruction repository
details: When Antigravity starts a conversation, it originally reads all SKILL.md files recursively, costing thousands of wasted context tokens. With the injector, stubs are read in the UI so the agent knows the skill exists, but it only triggers the 'mcp.fetch_skill' tool when a relevant action is requested, dynamically expanding the needed skill in the window.

name: saas-core
description: A highly scalable, production-ready SaaS template offering zero-config multi-tenant architecture, billing, usage limits, and background workflows.
keyFeatures: Dual auth runtime: seamless fallback or active selection between Clerk and Auth.js v5
Multi-tenant billing: Stripe subscriptions with usage-limit tracking and automatic pricing mapping
Background queuing: Trigger.dev background worker and cron job runners
Idempotent Bootstrapping: automated GitHub actions build/connect Vercel, Supabase, Redis, and Stripe on git push
GDPR compliance: self-serve user data export (/api/user/export) and user account deletion (/api/user/delete)
architecture:
graph TD
  Next[Next.js App Router] --> Prisma[Prisma ORM]
  Prisma --> DB[(Supabase Postgres)]
  Next --> Stripe[Stripe Billing]
  Next --> Trigger[Trigger.dev Background Jobs]
  Next --> Redis[(Upstash Redis Rate Limit)]
fileStructure: src/app/(dashboard)/ - Authenticated workspaces and user settings routes
src/app/api/stripe/webhook - Stripe subscription state synchronizer
src/lib/plans.ts - Plan tiers, Stripe pricing IDs, and usage limit variables
src/lib/rate-limit.ts - Redis-backed B2C and organization rate gates
prisma/schema.prisma - Full database schema (User, Org, Sub, Notification, File, Audit)
scripts/bootstrap.ts - Idempotent resource orchestrator script
details: Customizing the SaaS is done via saas-core.config.json (toggle features like stripe, jobs, slack) and service-config.ts (branding name, tagline, color). Database schema migrations are handled via 'prisma migrate dev', and local development is bootstrapped via 'npm run bootstrap:local'.

name: thesys-c1-dashboard
description: A developer-focused dashboard for monitoring and managing AI agent sessions, with rich telemetry for Generative UI pipelines using the Thesys C1 framework.
keyFeatures: Real-time Generative UI telemetry: tracks agent execution steps, schema rendering, and interface states
Session dashboard: lists user sessions, tool outputs, and LLM raw logs
Automated CI/CD: seamless GitHub Actions pipelines pushing code quality and Vercel deployments
Prebuilt widgets: standard components for visualizing agent run performance, latency, and success counts
architecture:
graph TD
  Agent[AI Agent] -->|Generative UI events| Thesys[Thesys C1 API]
  Thesys -->|Webhook| Dashboard[Next.js Developer Console]
  Dashboard --> Recharts[Telemetry Visuals]
fileStructure: src/app/dashboard/ - Layout, charts, and telemetry grids
src/components/telemetry/ - Recharts wraps and generative UI action steps cards
src/lib/thesys.ts - Webhook validator and Generative UI state mapper
.github/workflows/deploy.yml - Vercel automatic builder and deployment pipeline
details: Provides developers running generative UI agents in production with exact session replays, showing when UI elements were rendered, what JSON schemas were requested by the LLM, and how users interacted with the dynamic cards.
